item.title: T. Rowe’s Chung Sees Pullback in ‘Expensive’ Yuan After Rally
item.description: T. Rowe Price is betting on a pullback in the yuan, arguing that it looks “expensive” against a basket of currencies from China’s major trading partners.

item.title: Falling Inflation Expectations Take Pressure Off Bank of England
item.description: Inflation expectations among UK households followed oil prices lower in June, the latest sign the Bank of England may be able to avoid hiking interest rates.

item.title: Navis Explores Sale of Stake in Nuts and Snacks Firm Dan-D Foods
item.description: Navis Capital Partners is planning to sell its majority stake in nuts and snacks company Dan-D Foods Ltd.

item.title: China Gives Coal Room to Grow in New Five-Year Energy Plan
item.description: China is leaving room for coal consumption to grow in coming years, as the stability of the world’s largest energy market continues to outweigh climate concerns.

item.title: Key Kazakh Field Cuts Oil Output After Drones Hit Russian Plant
item.description: Kazakhstan’s giant Karachaganak oil and gas field cut crude production by more than a quarter after a drone attack forced the shutdown of a processing plant in Russia that handles its gas.

item.title: Emerging Market Stocks Slide Most in Three Weeks on Tech Selloff
item.description: Emerging-market stocks slumped the most in almost three weeks, dragged lower by a global tech selloff that hammered South Korean equities and triggered a 20-minute trading halt.

item.title: European Natural Gas Edges Higher as Traders Monitor Hormuz Risk
item.description: European natural gas prices inched higher after an attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz renewed fears about safe passage through the critical waterway.

item.title: Palm Oil Climbs on Higher Exports and Indian Demand Expectations
item.description: Palm oil advanced on rising Malaysian exports and expectations of better demand from the biggest buyer India as festival season nears.

item.title: Lescure Sticks With 5% French Deficit After Watchdog Rebuke
item.description: Finance Minister Roland Lescure reaffirmed his commitment to lower France’s deficit after the country’s fiscal watchdog rebuked the government’s pending plans.

item.title: Zalando Plunges as Regulator Probes Retailer’s Accounts
item.description: Zalando SE plunged after Germany’s financial regulator opened a probe into the online fashion retailer’s 2025 report over suspected violations of accounting rules.

item.title: Traffic Flows Through Hormuz Despite Shock Container Ship Attack
item.description: An attack on a container vessel sailing through the Strait of Hormuz has prompted some shipowners to review exit plans, but traffic continued to flow in both directions through the vital thoroughfare on Friday.

item.title: The AI Trade Is No Longer About Owning One Thing: Taking Stock
item.description: The case for the artificial-intelligence trade remains intact, but the risk of getting it wrong has risen considerably. This week showed just how unforgiving the theme has become.

item.title: FTSE 100 Futures Fall as Tech Weakness Hits Sentiment
PBOC May Set New Overnight Operation Rate at 1.35%, Survey Shows
item.description: China’s central bank is expected to price its inaugural overnight reverse repo operation slightly below the seven-day rate when it debuts the tool on Monday, a Bloomberg survey shows.

item.title: Demant’s Latest AI Hearing Aid Fuels Best Quarter in 26 Years
item.description: The rollout of a new hearing aid equipped with artificial intelligence is driving the shares of Denmark’s Demant A/S to their best quarterly performance since 2000.

item.title: RBI Proposes Wider Access to Indian Money Markets in Draft Rules
item.description: India’s central bank has proposed broader participation in the country’s money market to include shadow lenders, companies and other financial institutions, in a move aimed at deepening funding markets and expanding access to short-term liquidity.

item.title: Apple Price Hikes Spark Asia Tech Selloff on Memory Cost Concern
item.description: Asian technology stocks slumped after Apple raised prices for its products, stoking concern that rising component prices will curb demand for devices and eventually slow the memory chip rally that has powered much of the AI trade.

item.title: GCash Owner Seeks Up to $1.5 Billion in Record Philippine IPO
item.description: The company behind the Philippines’ biggest mobile wallet GCash is preparing what may become the country’s biggest initial public offering ever, seeking to raise as much as 92.3 billion pesos ($1.5 billion).

item.title: Naples Outpaces Milan as Southern Italy Drives Economic Growth
item.description: The south has historically lagged the north — but that’s changing.

item.title: Burnham Inherits a UK Economy That’s Just Starting to Look Up
item.description: As Keir Starmer was reeling from heavy local election losses last month, it was hard to see why anyone would be trying to take over the embattled prime minister’s job.

item.title: Canada’s Quebecor Makes Last Push to Thwart Corus Debt Swap
item.description: Quebecor Inc. urged Canada’s broadcasting regulator to hold a public hearing on the proposed takeover of Corus Entertainment Inc. by its lenders — arguing that a better option is to allow Quebecor to buy the heavily indebted media company.

item.title: Drone Startup Elroy Air Is Said to Near $800 Million SPAC Deal
item.description: Elroy Air Inc., a cargo drone startup aiming to replace delivery trucks, is in advanced talks to go public through a merger with a blank-check vehicle that will create a company with an enterprise value of about $1 billion.

item.title: Empty LNG Tankers Mass Outside Qatar as Exports Tick Higher
item.description: Empty liquefied natural gas tankers are lining up outside of Qatar’s massive export plant in the Persian Gulf as the supplier seeks to quickly increase production following early progress in US-Iran peace talks.

item.title: China’s Collapsing Crude Oil Imports to Fall Further in June
item.description: The world’s top crude oil importer is set to take even fewer barrels this month, extending its exceptionally subdued appetite since the start of the Iran war.

item.title: Aluminum Heads for Fourth Weekly Drop on Mideast Supply Return
item.description: Aluminum headed for a fourth weekly decline, the longest losing streak since April 2025, as a renewed selloff in Asian tech stocks added to pressure from a stronger US dollar and returning Middle East supplies.

item.title: Philippines, UAE and Indonesia to Start CPTPP Accession Talks
item.description: The Philippines, United Arab Emirates and Indonesia will start talks about joining a regional trade bloc, the next step for the three in efforts to expand free-trade ties.

item.title: Hong Kong Dollar Hits 10-Month Low as Fed View Buoys Greenback
item.description: The Hong Kong dollar fell to its weakest level against the US dollar in about 10 months, as a stronger greenback and expectations for further Federal Reserve rate hikes pressured Asian currencies.

item.title: Korean Stocks Tumble 8% as Chipmakers Slide, Trading Halted
item.description: South Korean stocks fell as renewed selling in chipmakers highlighted their heightened sensitivity to swings in global artificial-intelligence sentiment.

item.title: Iron Ore Set for Seventh Straight Weekly Loss as Market Softens
item.description: Iron ore headed for a seventh weekly loss — the worst run since 2022 — as demand weakened seasonally and mill margins narrowed.

item.title: Sherritt Warns of Going-Concern Risk After Trump Cuba Sanctions
item.description: A Canadian miner that is one of Cuba’s biggest foreign investors warned investors that its ability to carry on as a going concern is in doubt after Donald Trump expanded US sanctions on the Caribbean nation.
